provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Fire and Rescue (General) Regulations, 2008.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Fire and Rescue Force Act; [ Act No. 14 of 2007 ] " appointment " includes appointment into office, promotion and transfer; " fire and rescue officer " means a member of the Force but does not include a recruit; " Force " means the Fire and Rescue Force; " non-commissioned officer " means a fire and rescue officer of the rank of sergeant major, sergeant or corporal; " officer-in-charge " means a fire and rescue officer appointed by the Commissioner-General to be in-charge of a fire station; Part II – Constitution, administration and duties of the Force

provisions.title: Constitution of the Force
provisions.content: The Force shall consist of— (a) gazetted officers as follows— (i) Commissioner General; (ii) Deputy Commissioner General; (iii) Commissioner; (iv) Deputy Commissioner; (v) Senior Assistant Commissioner; (vi) Assistant Commissioner; (vii) Senior Superintendent; (viii) Superintendent; and (ix) Assistant Superintendent; (b) Inspectors and Assistant Inspectors; (c) non-commissioned officers as follows: (i) Sergeant Major; (ii) Station Sergeant; (iii) Sergeant; (iv) Corporal; and (v) Constable.

provisions.title: Categories of stations
provisions.content: (1) The Force shall consist of fire and rescue stations of the following categories— (a) city, municipal and town Stations; (b) airport stations and; (c) port stations (2) Fire and rescue stations shall be as set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations provided that the Commissioner-General may from time to time direct the establishment of new stations as circumstances may dictate. (3) The Commissioner General shall designate specific areas to be the headquarters of stations.

provisions.title: Director­ates in the Force
provisions.content: (1) The force shall be composed of such directorates as follows— (a) finance and administration directorate, administration and resource management; (b) supervision and monitoring directorate operations Division; (c) policy, information and research directorate; and (d) fire, safety and inspection directorate fire safety Division. (2) Every directorate under sub-regulation (1) shall be headed by a Commissioner.

provisions.title: Regional fire and rescue office etc.
provisions.content: A Regional Fire and Rescue Office shall be headed by a fire and rescue officer of or above the rank of Senior Assistant Commissioner as the Commissioner-General may appoint, and such officer shall be designated as Regional Fire and Rescue Officer. (2) A District Fire and Rescue Office shall be headed by a fire and rescue officer of or above the rank of Superintendent as the Commissioner­ General may appoint and such officer shall be designated as District Fire and Rescue Officer. (3) Every fire station shall be under the command of a senior officer as the Commissioner may appoint and such officer shall be designated as officer-in-charge of a station.

provisions.title: Entry qualifica­tions and duties of Con­stable
provisions.content: (1) A person may be employed as a Constable if the person— (a) is a form four leaver with not less than division four and has successfully completed a basic course on fire and rescue management for the period of not less than nine months; (b) is a holder of a full technician certificate or equivalent qualifications from a recognized institution and has successful completed a basic course in fire and rescue management; or (c) is an employee of the Force in other cadres and has successfully completed a nine months basic course in fire and rescue management. (2) The duties of the Constable shall be to— (a) extinguish fire; (b) save life and property; (c) attend fire and emergency calls; (d) operate fire pumps; (e) render humanitarian services; (f) maintain and inspect fire equipments; (g) maintain security of work place; (h) operate fire watch room and control room; (i) attend routine and ceremonial guards, and (j) discharge any other duty as may be assigned to him by his officer in-charge.

provisions.title: Qualifi­cations and duties of Corporal
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Corporal if the person— (a) Has held a post of Constable for the period of three years and has successfully completed the basic course in fire and rescue management; or (b) Is employed or re-categorized from another cadre and has successfully completed the basic fire and rescue management course; (2) The duties of the Corporal shall be the same as the duties of the Constable and in addition he shall— (a) supervise constables on routine duties; (b) ensure that all fire equipment under his control are in good working order and stowage; (c) maintain discipline and ensure proper performance of all firemen under his command; (d) maintain security of work place; and (e) Conduct fire drills and exercises.

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions and duties of Sergeant
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Sergeant if the person— (a) is a form six leaver who has successfully completed basic fire and rescue management course and fire and rescue leadership course in accordance with the approved curricula; or (b) has held a post of fire and rescue corporal for the period of three years and has successful completed a six months course in fire and rescue leadership in accordance with the approved curricula. (2) The duties of the Sergeant shall be the same as the duties of the Corporal and in addition he shall— (a) manage fire and rescue services and activities as may be prescribed by the Commissioner General; (b) detail fire and rescue duties; (c) prepare and conduct station training; and (d) discharge any other duty as may be assigned to him by his officer in-charge.

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions and duties of Sergeant Major
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Sergeant Major if the person has held a post of station Sergeant with a proven satisfactory service and conduct with leadership ability. (2) The duties of the Sergeant Major shall be to— (a) maintain discipline and sanitation in the camp; (b) discharge any other duty as may be assigned to him by his senior officers

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions and duties of Assistant Inspector
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Assistant Inspector if the person— (a) is the holder of a degree or advanced diploma and who has successfully completed basic fire and rescue management course and inspector's course in accordance with the approved curricula; or (b) has held a post of Station Sergeant for a period of three years and has successfully completed inspector's course in accordance with the approved curricula. (2) The duties of the Assistant Inspector shall be the same as the duties of the sergeant Major and in addition be shall— (a) supervise fire fighting operations; (b) ensure service and ability of fire equipments under his control; (c) ensure immediate safe disposal of casualties and render first aid treatment to them; (d) inspect and provide consultation regarding fire and protection to airports, towns, ports and villages; and (e) discharge other duty as may be assigned to him by his officer in-charge;

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions and duties of Inspector
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Inspector if the person has hold a post of Assistant inspector for the period of three years and has a good conduct with high degree of command. (2) The duties of the Inspector shall be the same as the duties of Assistant Inspector and in addition he shall— (a) prepare and conduct fire prevention and protection training to the public; (b) inspect and approve layout and diagrams of buildings including factories; (c) carry out fire investigation; (d) keep records of fire occurrences, disasters and losses; and (e) discharge any other duty as may be assigned to him by his officer in-charge.

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions and duties of the Fire and Rescue Assistant Superintendent
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Assistant Superintendent if the person has— (a) held a post of Inspector for the period of three years; (b) good conduct and high degree of command; and (c) successfully completed a three months course for senior fire and rescue officers offered within the country or abroad. (2) The duties of the Assistant Superintendent shall be the same as the duties of the Inspector and in addition he shall discharge any other duty as may be assigned to him by his senior officer

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions and duties of the Superin­tendent
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Superintendent if the person has— (a) held a post of Assistant Superintendent for the period of three years; and (b) good conduct and leadership ability. (2) The duties of the Superintendent shall be the same as the duties of the Assistant Superintendent and in addition he shall discharge any other duty as may be assigned to him by his senior officer

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions of Assistant Com­missioner
provisions.content: A person may hold a post of Assistant Commissioner if the person has held a post of Senior Superintendent for the period of three years.

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions and duties of Senior Superin­tendent
provisions.content: (1) A person may hold a post of Senior Superintendent if the person has— (a) held a post of superintendent for the period of three years satisfactory services; (b) good conduct and leadership ability. (2) The duties of the Senior superintendent shall be the same as the duties of the superintendent and in addition he shall discharge any other duty as may be assigned to him by his senior officer. (3) May be appointed officer in charge of category seven airports of municipal or ports. (4) May be appointed Regional fire Officer or head of the section.

provisions.title: Qualifications of Senior Assistant Commissioner
provisions.content: A person-may hold a post of Senior Assistant Commissioner if the person has held a post of Assistant Commissioner for the period of three years.

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions of Deputy Com­missioner
provisions.content: A person may hold a post of Deputy Commissioner if the person has held a post of Senior Assistant Commissioner for the period of three years.

provisions.title: Qualifica­tions of Com­missioner
provisions.content: The person may hold a post of Commissioner if the person has held a post of Deputy Commissioner.

provisions.title: Declara­tion
provisions.content: A person who is enlisted in the Force shall declare his faithful and allegiance to the President of the United Republic in the manner prescribed in the Second Schedule.

provisions.title: Certificate of discharge
provisions.content: A person who is discharged from the Force shall be issued with a certificate of discharge as prescribed in the Second Schedule to these Regulations.

provisions.title: Exemp­tion
provisions.content: Notwithstanding any provisions of these regulations, an appointing authority may appoint an officer to a rank other than in the manner specified in these regulations. Part III – Dress regulations

provisions.title: Wearing of uniforms
provisions.content: (1) A fire and rescue officer shall, unless otherwise authorized wear uniform as prescribed in the Third Schedule to these regulations. (2) A fire and rescue officer shall not wear uniform or part of uniform while he is off duty. (3) Where a fire and rescue officer is promoted to the rank of Assistant Inspector, he shall continue to wear the uniforms he had before the promotion until he receives the appropriate uniforms.

provisions.title: Wearing of chevrons
provisions.content: (1) A fire and rescue officer shall wear the approved chevron on the upper part of the right sleeve at a midway point between the shoulder and elbow. (2) The chevron shall be pinned on the sleeve of the tunic blouse, shirt or jersey and the point of the chevron shall be downwards.

provisions.title: Wearing of lanyard and whistle kit inspection
provisions.content: (1) The red cord lanyard shall be worn round the left shoulder under the epaulette and across the left hand pocket ending in the whistle pocket. (2) A fire and rescue officer not on duty at a fire and rescue station shall submit his whistle kit for inspection on last Friday of each quarter of the year and the kit shall be inspected by the Senior Officer of the Station.

provisions.title: Wearing of buttons
provisions.content: (1) Medium size buttons shall be worn between the shoulder straps and pockets closing edge of tunic and shirts. (2) Small size buttons shall be used on pockets, shoulder straps, gorgets and caps.

provisions.title: Badges of rank
provisions.content: (1) Badges of rank shall, except chevrons and other metal badges, be worn on a black or green cloth background cut to the shape of the badge except where worn on blue epaulettes. (2) Badges shall be worn on— (a) the peaked cap - medium size; (b) the hat, double terrain - medium size; (c) the beret-medium size; (d) the fire and rescue helmet - medium size; (e) the mess dress lapel - small size; and (f) the collar of No. 1 dress tunic - small size for gazetted officers only.

provisions.title: Cere­monial dress
provisions.content: All fire and rescue officers of and above the rank of Sergeant Major shall wear ceremonial dress.
